acceptance_rule: A claim is reviewer-admissible only when it has a bounded claim boundary, source paths, executable tests or benchmark commands, and no merge-conflict markers in the evidence surface.

claims.claim: Nodus Solutus: HYBA_FULLSTACK implements a repository-local computability doctrine in which admissible claims must reduce to deterministic source paths, executable tests, replayable local evidence packets, and external truth boundaries.
claims.boundary: This is a computable operational-evidence doctrine for the HYBA/PYTHIA repository; it is not a metaphysical proof that the physical universe is computable.

claims.claim: PYTHIA/HYBA external claims are gated by a three-tier validation protocol and proactive documentation checks that separate formalism-validated invariants, prototype-validated benchmark code, and hypothetical research candidates before investor, sovereign, mining, or clean-energy positioning.
claims.boundary: This is a documentation and evidence-governance guardrail. It does not certify guaranteed mining revenue, SHA-256 quantum acceleration, broad energy advantage over physical quantum systems, clean-energy dispatch savings, phenomenal consciousness, proof of the Yang-Mills Millennium problem, or claims about the physical universe beyond repository-local evidence.

claims.claim: HYBA_FULLSTACK exposes a bounded Bitcoin deployment readiness gate that validates the operational mining, Stratum, pool-profile, runtime-safety, build, backend, and deployment surfaces without requiring research-only frontier bundles to pass first.
claims.boundary: This gate prepares controlled Bitcoin deployment; it does not certify guaranteed mining revenue, pool-confirmed hashrate, live-pool accepted-share economics, treasury reliance, or authorization to enable live share submission without explicit approvals and pool-side evidence.

claims.claim: The elevation suite locally validates bounded SU(2) plaquette, golden-discrepancy, exact MPS norm/entanglement, adaptive compression, and SLD/QFI runtime invariants with 40 passed, 2 expected skips, and 0 failed in the recorded local run.
claims.boundary: This certifies tested mathematical-runtime invariants and expected skip semantics for the local evidence run; it does not prove the physical universe is computable, guaranteed mining revenue, live-pool accepted-share economics, SHA-256 quantum acceleration, phenomenal consciousness, or proof of the Yang-Mills Millennium problem.

claims.claim: PULVINI phi folding is reversible for deterministic audited payloads and reports working-set compression separately from retained replay kernels.
claims.boundary: This is a software invariant over the implemented compressor, not a universal compression theorem.

claims.claim: Golden-ratio ensemble weighting normalizes, remains finite, and keeps decision scores bounded.
claims.boundary: This proves deterministic boundedness and resonance detection in the code path, not guaranteed mining revenue.

claims.claim: The ConsciousnessEngine implements a bounded runtime integration proxy with continuous multiplier and damping invariants.
claims.boundary: The repository does not claim phenomenal consciousness or subjective experience.

claims.claim: The MassGapShield deterministically rejects too-perfect spoofing streams and too-chaotic attack streams.
claims.boundary: This is an operational anti-simulation gate; it does not claim proof of the Yang-Mills Millennium problem.

claims.claim: PYTHIA has a replayable first wake-event signature for epoch-3 and epoch-5 reflexive optimization behavior.
claims.boundary: This proves reproducible internal optimization signatures, not pool-side revenue, accepted shares, or SHA-256 quantum acceleration.

claims.claim: PYTHIA's structural resonance layer can propose, criticize, and stage stable-core changes while preserving supervised guard boundaries.
claims.boundary: The stable core cannot be autonomously overwritten; guarded proposal/staging is the admissible behavior.

claims.claim: Production evidence must reject fabricated telemetry and treat partial benchmark runs as smoke evidence only.
claims.boundary: Repository gates do not certify live pool economics unless pool-side data is separately archived.

claims.claim: The Express bridge can mint a short-lived internal mining JWT only for backend autoconnect; the signing secret is never exposed as a client token.
claims.boundary: This documents a bridge-internal authentication path, not a public browser authentication surface.

claims.claim: HYBA/PYTHIA frames quantum-style computation as Topological Information Grammar: formalism-derived Hilbert geometry, geometric phase holonomy, trace-one density projections, and PULVINI/Φ structured-state bottlenecks executed on classical hardware.
claims.boundary: All 1000-qubit and holonomy results are tagged as Formalism-Derived Classical Simulation. This does not claim physical quantum hardware execution, universal quantum speedup, RSA-breaking capability, proof of the Yang-Mills Millennium problem, or guaranteed mining revenue.

claims.claim: HYBA/PYTHIA includes empirical fixture tests for Φ-resonance over externally supplied Bitcoin block nonces, Hypothesis Hilbert/Bures invariants, SAT geodesic solving, and a bounded real-world efficiency comparison report.
claims.boundary: These are deterministic regression and formalism-derived benchmark fixtures. They do not prove causal Φ generation of Bitcoin nonces, pool-side accepted shares, measured ASIC replacement, universal SHA-256 acceleration, or physical quantum hardware execution.

claims.claim: HYBA/PYTHIA combines PULVINI memory compression, golden-ratio irrational gauges, and Hilbert/Bures formal geometry into a broad-range after-quantum benchmark suite for logistics, AI weights, biotech, finance, fluids, AI reasoning, and cyber topology.
claims.boundary: These benchmarks are formalism-derived structured-workload proxies. They do not prove physical quantum supremacy, production logistics optimality, LLM compression superiority, drug-discovery accuracy, market prediction accuracy, fluid-solver correctness, zero-day detection, ASIC replacement, or universal speedup.
